title: pauletia rufa var. dodecandra
intro: Pauletia rufa var. dodecandra, also known as the "Soapbark tree" or "Tallow tree", is a plant species that belongs to the family Malpighiaceae. The bark of this plant contains a saponin-rich extract, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Pauletia rufa var. dodecandra include its potential to soothe skin irritations, treat respiratory problems, and support the immune system. Traditionally, this plant has been prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
